blocks.name: Mouse and Keyboard Guide
blocks.description:
Welcome to OSDev Visual Studio!

- Move Block: Left-click and drag a block.
- Pan Canvas: Click and drag with the Middle Mouse Button.
- Snap to Grid: Hold Alt while dragging a block to snap it to the 40px grid.
- Multi-Select: Left-click and drag on empty canvas to draw a selection box.
- Connect Ports: Click and drag from an output port (right) to an input port (left).
- Edit Properties: Right-click a block.
- Delete: Select a block or connection and press Delete or Backspace.
- Copy/Paste: Select block(s), press Ctrl+C, then Ctrl+V.
- Toggle Code Preview: Click 'Toggle Code Preview' in the top toolbar.

blocks.name: Boot
blocks.description: The entry point of the OS. The bootloader jumps here. It initializes the VGA text buffer and starts execution. Only one Boot block is allowed per project.

blocks.name: Print
blocks.description: Outputs text to the screen using VGA text mode. Uses the currently active Text Color and BG Color.

blocks.name: Text Color
blocks.description: Sets the foreground color for all subsequent Print blocks.

blocks.name: BG Color
blocks.description: Sets the background color for all subsequent Print blocks.

blocks.name: Clear Screen
blocks.description: Wipes all text from the VGA buffer and resets the cursor to the top left.

blocks.name: Loop
blocks.description: Repeats the execution flow connected to its 'body' port N times.

blocks.name: If
blocks.description: Branches execution. If the condition evaluates to non-zero, the 'true' branch executes. Otherwise, the 'false' branch executes.

blocks.name: If Variable
blocks.description: Branches execution by comparing a variable to a value.

blocks.name: Go to
blocks.description: Jumps execution directly to the block connected to its output. The target block will have a label generated above it in the C code.

blocks.name: Trigger (Cycles)
blocks.description: Runs independently in the background. Executes its connected chain every X CPU cycles. Does not need to be attached to the Boot block.

blocks.name: Wait
blocks.description: Pauses OS execution for a specified number of CPU cycles.

blocks.name: Memory
blocks.description: Allocates or frees a block of memory using a simple bump allocator.

blocks.name: Interrupt
blocks.description: Registers an Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) for hardware events like the timer or keyboard.

blocks.name: Declare Constant
blocks.description: Declares an integer variable with an initial value.

blocks.name: Set Variable
blocks.description: Updates an integer variable with a value.

blocks.name: Get Variable
blocks.description: Placeholder block to remind you a variable exists.

blocks.name: Add
blocks.description: Adds A and B. Result is stored in a variable.

blocks.name: Subtract
blocks.description: Subtracts B from A. Result is stored in a variable.

blocks.name: Multiply
blocks.description: Multiplies A and B. Result is stored in a variable.

blocks.name: Divide
blocks.description: Divides A by B. Result is stored in a variable.

blocks.name: Compare
blocks.description: Compares A and B using an operator (==, !=, >, <). Result is 1 (true) or 0 (false). Can be fed to an If block.

blocks.name: Read String
blocks.description: Stops execution and reads a string from the keyboard. Stores it in a variable.

blocks.name: Read Int
blocks.description: Stops execution and reads an integer from the keyboard. Stores it in a variable.

blocks.name: Print Number
blocks.description: Prints the value of an integer variable to the screen.

blocks.name: Custom Code
blocks.description: Allows you to type raw C or Assembly code. Double-click the block to open the editor.

blocks.name: Comment
blocks.description: A sticky note for leaving reminders or explanations. Does not generate any code. Scales to fit your text.
